name: canopy CLI surface
description: Warren's canopy facade shells out to two cn subcommands: 'cn list --tag agent --json' (returns the {success, command, prompts:[{name,version,status,tags}]} envelope filtered to active prompts) and 'cn render <name> --json' (returns {success, command, name, version, sections:[{name,body}], resolvedFrom, frontmatter}). The global --json flag is the right one — 'cn render --format json' emits a bare object without success/command, which makes auth-vs-failure disambiguation lossy. Failed renders return {success:false, command:'render', error:<msg>} on stdout AND exit 1; the warren facade peeks for the structured error envelope before falling back to exit-code interpretation.

title: Filter agents by canopy tag 'agent', not by frontmatter
rationale: Warren SPEC §2.1 wording is 'agent: true schema tag' which reads ambiguous. Canopy supports both freeform tags (string[]) and frontmatter (Record<string, unknown>); 'cn list --tag agent --json' is the supported CLI filter and matches the example in canopy SPEC.md line 73 ('tags: ["agent", "leaf"]'). Picking tag-based filtering keeps warren aligned with the canopy CLI surface — operators use 'cn create --tag agent' to register an agent, not a frontmatter field.

content: Required sections for warren's agent definition schema: 'system' is the only required section. Optional sections from SPEC §4.2 (skills, expertise_seed, burrow_config, workflow) are consumed at run-spawn time (Phase 5) — Phase 3's schema does not require them. Canopy's per-prompt --schema rules are the right place to enforce richer structural constraints; warren only enforces the minimum needed to spawn an agent.

name: Per-agent error isolation in registry refresh
description: registry.refreshAgentRegistry collects per-prompt failures into RefreshResult.skipped instead of throwing — one bad prompt (missing 'system' section, archived between list and render, etc.) shouldn't block the other 19 from registering. Transport-layer failures (cn binary missing, canopy clone unreachable) DO abort the whole refresh because there's nothing useful to partially register if the registry is unreadable. Mirrors the burrow-client transport-error mapping pattern: per-call structured errors flow through; per-channel failures throw CanopyUnavailableError.

title: Hard-reset to origin/HEAD on every refresh, no merge
rationale: Warren's canopy clone is a cache, not a working tree. Operators edit prompts in their own checkout, push to GitHub, then refresh. A regular 'git pull' can fail with conflicts if anything stray landed in the warren-side clone (half-finished cn write, file-mode drift), so cloneOrUpdateCanopyRepo does 'git fetch --prune' + 'git reset --hard origin/<HEAD>'. Default branch is auto-detected via 'git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD' with a 'git remote set-head --auto' fallback, so most repos work without configuration.

content: Warren env vars for canopy registry are warren-namespaced AND keep CANOPY_REPO_URL un-prefixed: CANOPY_REPO_URL (the operator's library URL — required, no default), WARREN_CANOPY_DIR (local clone path, default /data/canopy-repo), WARREN_CN_BINARY (default 'cn'), WARREN_GIT_BINARY (default 'git'). CANOPY_REPO_URL is the canonical name in SPEC §10.2 and matches how the operator sets it via 'fly secrets set CANOPY_REPO_URL=...' — keep it un-prefixed even though warren-side configs are normally WARREN_*.

Canopy claude-code prompt (canopy-daf3) needs an explicit commit clause: 'commit your changes; warren handles the push, you do not need to push it yourself'. Validated 2026-05-09 (run_agpet4ev6e4a): adding the clause produced ahead_by: 1 with a real diff. Do NOT instruct the agent to push — agent-side git push fails inside the sandbox (warren-1a09: /root/.gitconfig invisible to bwrap, GITHUB_TOKEN not env-passthroughed). Warren reap is the actual push mechanism (host-side, sees /root/.gitconfig). Without the commit clause, runs silently no-op (run_a98cfx1fantf: 49 turns, ahead_by: 0, branchPushed: true).

canopy claude-code (canopy-daf3) is now the sandbox operating contract: workspace map (.canopy/agent.json, .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl, .seeds/issues.jsonl), rituals (ml prime, sd ready/close, quality gates, ml record/sd close at terminal turn), steering protocol ([STEERING] user turns are priority), expectations (no opportunistic refactors, never --no-verify, network governed by burrow_config, commit-only — warren reaps and pushes per warren-1a09). The canopy library lives at github.com/jayminwest/understory.git (renamed from my-canopy).

title: CANOPY_REPO_URL is optional; warren ships built-in agents inline
rationale: First-time install required cloning a canopy library, which forced operators into a 'fork understory, set CANOPY_REPO_URL, refresh' yak-shave just to dispatch a one-line claude-code prompt. Built-ins (claude-code, sapling) at src/registry/builtins/ seed the agents table on every server boot (and in 'warren run'); a configured canopy library still loads additively over them via upsert, so library agents override built-ins by name. Provenance flows through frontmatter.source: 'builtin'; GET /agents reads it to surface source per row. doctor / readyz canopy probes degrade to informational ok:true when CANOPY_REPO_URL is unset; POST /agents/refresh and 'warren register-agent' 400 with a friendly hint.

content: Built-in agents encode provenance via renderedJson.frontmatter.source = 'builtin'; canopy-loaded agents leave it unset (canopy doesn't emit it). readAgentSource() in src/registry/builtins/index.ts is the single reader; the HTTP /agents endpoint uses it to attach a 'source' field per row.

seedBuiltinAgents preserves existing rows: if a row already exists for a builtin name (whether seeded earlier or upserted via canopy refresh), it is skipped — never overwritten. This protects library overrides across server restarts.

warren-1a09 (sandbox push has no auth path) is resolved at the contract layer, not the auth layer: V1 ships fix A (canopy claude-code/sapling builtins instruct 'Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream' + 'Do not run git push yourself' in src/registry/builtins/{claude-code,sapling}.ts) and fix D (SPEC.md:150 §4.3 step 6 documents the agent-commits/reap-pushes contract with warren-1a09 reference and bwrap /root invisibility explanation). Fix B (relocate insteadOf rule into bwrap ro-bind set, e.g. /etc/gitconfig) and fix C (env-passthrough GITHUB_TOKEN) are deferred — only revisit when V2 needs sandbox-side push (gh pr create, fork pushes, multi-remote). Token exposure is the primary tradeoff for B/C: the rewrite URL embeds GITHUB_TOKEN in plaintext, so any agent reading /etc/gitconfig extracts it.

title: Cross-repo coordination via warren step-0 seed gates downstream warren work on burrow merge
rationale: When a warren plan step depends on a runtime contract that lives in burrow's AgentRegistry, model it as a coordination-only seed (warren-0e06 pattern): the seed body pins the contract (id, supportsResume, spawn shape, RPC commands, event kinds, envPassthrough) and links to burrow's tracking seed + plan. The warren seed stays open until burrow ships a tagged release containing the runtime AND a cross-repo PR link is recorded back into the warren seed. Closing before burrow ships unblocks downstream warren work (the pi builtin add, prepare-workspace seeding, multi-provider surface, cost columns) that would then hard-fail with NotFoundError('agent runtime not registered: pi') at dispatch — the warren->burrow runtime-id forwarding contract (mx-7352f4, mx-b0a02c, mx-c8fc41) gives no fallback. The seed acts as both a contract-spec artifact and a tombstone preventing premature closure of dependent steps.

name: pi runtime contract (warren-side expectations on burrow piRuntime)
description: Pinned via warren-0e06 / burrow-8aff cross-repo coordination. Warren forwards agent.name='pi' as the burrow runtime id (mx-7352f4) and seeds workspaces via agents:['pi'] in POST /burrows (mx-c8fc41); burrow's piRuntime must register accordingly. Contract: id='pi'; displayName='Pi'; supportsResume=true (pi --continue / --session); spawn shape 'pi --mode rpc --no-session [--no-extensions] --provider <…> --model <…>' (RPC mode, not --mode json — pl-4374 alt #2 chose RPC for the bidirectional steer/abort/get_state/get_session_stats surface that warren's spawn-per-turn flow assumes); RPC commands consumed by warren: prompt, steer, abort, get_state, get_session_stats; event kinds parseEvents must surface: agent_start, agent_end, turn_start, turn_end, message_start, message_update, message_end, tool_execution_start, tool_execution_update, tool_execution_end, queue_update, compaction_start, compaction_end, auto_retry_start, auto_retry_end, extension_error (warren-70af UI renderer expects this superset; unknown kinds fall through generic); envPassthrough widens beyond claude-code's ANTHROPIC_* + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN set to include OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, PI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY (warren-f8c0 multi-provider depends on this — implemented at the runtime contract layer, not via docker-compose.yml, per burrow's envPassthrough escape hatch); installCheck via 'pi --version' (runVersionCheck shape). If burrow ships piRuntime with a different id (e.g. 'pi-coding-agent'), update warren's BUILTIN_AGENTS name in lockstep — the two strings must match exactly.

content: AgentDefinition.frontmatter.provider and .model are the well-known optional string fields for the multi-provider surface (warren-f8c0). They live in the open frontmatter bag — no schema rev. readProviderFrontmatter() narrows them to typed strings; withProviderOverrides() folds operator-supplied per-run overrides onto a freshly-cloned AgentDefinition before spawnRun freezes runs.rendered_agent_json. Empty/whitespace overrides are no-ops; the base agent row is untouched (override is per-run, not per-agent).

Adding a new built-in agent (alongside claude-code/sapling/pi) requires updates in five places in lockstep: (1) src/registry/builtins/<name>.ts with the AgentDefinition (mirror SAPLING_BUILTIN shape: name, version=1, sections.system + sections.burrow_config, resolvedFrom=['builtin:<name>'], frontmatter.source='builtin'); (2) src/registry/builtins/index.ts BUILTIN_AGENTS array + re-export; (3) src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts assertions on BUILTIN_AGENT_NAMES + readAgentSource; (4) scripts/acceptance/scenarios/13-container-smoke.ts builtin-names check; (5) src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx header + empty-state copy. Note: scenario 02-agents-refresh.ts checks 'no library-sourced agents before refresh' via filter source !== 'builtin', so it auto-extends without a code change.

PI_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/pi.ts) ships the same parity shape as SAPLING_BUILTIN (system + burrow_config sections, network='open'). The pi-specific surfaces — multi-provider override (frontmatter.provider/model), cost tracking (runs.cost_usd), .pi/skills + .pi/prompts materialization, pi-namespaced event kinds — are layered in follow-on steps of pl-4374 (warren-846b/f8c0/a7dc/70af/f1da), not in this builtin file.
